description: Mandatory structure for Informed Consent Form per ICH E6(R3) Section 4.8 and NDCTR 2019 Rule 18
language_style: Plain language, second person (you), active voice

subsections: Study Title (in plain language)
Why is this study being done?
Who is sponsoring this study?
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.10(a). Must explain purpose in simple terms understandable to layperson.
sections.special_instructions: Avoid medical jargon. Use analogies if helpful. Example: 'This study will test if Medicine X works better than Medicine Y for treating diabetes.'

subsections: What will happen if you take part?
Study visits and tests
How long will you be in the study?
Can you stop being in the study?
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.10(b). Must describe all procedures, including randomization, blinding, and placebo use in plain language.
sections.special_instructions: Use bullet points for visit schedule. Explain randomization as 'like flipping a coin'. Specify total time commitment.

subsections: What are the risks of being in this study?
Known side effects of the study medicine
Unknown risks
Risks to pregnancy and breastfeeding (if applicable)
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.10(d) and NDCTR 2019 Rule 18(2)(d). Must list all reasonably foreseeable risks, including reproductive risks.
sections.special_instructions: List common, serious, and unknown risks separately. Use frequency terms: very common (>10%), common (1-10%), uncommon (<1%).

subsections: Will you benefit from being in this study?
Are there benefits to others?
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.10(e). Must be realistic. Do NOT promise therapeutic benefit if not expected.
sections.special_instructions: If no direct benefit expected, state clearly: 'You may not receive any direct benefit from being in this study. The information learned may help future patients.'

subsections: What are your other choices if you do not join this study?
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.10(f). Must list available alternative treatments, including standard of care.
sections.special_instructions: List standard treatments available in India. Include option of no treatment if medically appropriate.

subsections: How will your information be protected?
Who will see your information?
How long will your information be kept?
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.10(h) and IT Act 2000. Must explain data protection measures and who has access.
sections.special_instructions: Mention regulatory authorities (CDSCO), ethics committee, and sponsor access. State retention period (minimum 3 years per NDCTR 2019).

subsections: What happens if you are injured because of the study?
What compensation will you receive?
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per NDCTR 2019 Rule 11 and Schedule VI. MANDATORY for India. Must specify insurance coverage and compensation mechanism.
sections.special_instructions: Include insurance policy details. State: 'The sponsor has provided insurance to cover medical treatment for study-related injuries.' Specify no-fault compensation.

subsections: Will you have to pay for anything?
Will you be paid for being in this study?
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.10(g). Must clearly state what is provided free (study medicine, tests) and any reimbursement for time/travel.
sections.special_instructions: State clearly: 'You will not have to pay for the study medicine or study-related tests.' Specify reimbursement amount if applicable.

subsections: Do you have to be in this study?
Can you leave the study?
What happens if you leave?
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.10(i). CRITICAL: Must emphasize voluntary nature and right to withdraw without penalty.
sections.special_instructions: Use strong language: 'Your participation is completely voluntary. You can refuse to join or leave at any time without giving a reason. This will not affect your medical care.'

subsections: Who can you contact if you have questions?
Study doctor contact
Ethics committee contact
Sponsor contact
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.10(j) and NDCTR 2019 Rule 18(2)(j). Must provide 24-hour contact for study doctor.
sections.special_instructions: Include: Study doctor name and phone (24-hour), EC name and contact, Sponsor representative. Use placeholder: [SITE TO COMPLETE: investigator contact details]

subsections: Consent statement
Subject signature and date
Legally acceptable representative signature (if applicable)
Person obtaining consent signature and date
Witness signature (if applicable)
sections.regulatory_requirements: Per ICH E6(R3) 4.8.9 and NDCTR 2019 Rule 18(3). Must include statement that subject received copy of ICF.
sections.special_instructions: Include statement: 'I have read this consent form (or it has been read to me). I have had the chance to ask questions. I agree to take part in this study.' Provide signature blocks for all required parties.

mandatory_elements_checklist: Expected benefits (or statement of no direct benefit)
